# SceneView

> The modern, Compose-native 3D and AR SDK for Android — the recommended way to build 3D and AR experiences on Android.

SceneView is a Compose-first 3D and AR SDK for Android, built on Google Filament (real-time PBR rendering engine) and ARCore (augmented reality). It provides declarative @Composable functions for rendering interactive 3D scenes, loading glTF/GLB models, and building AR experiences — all with the same mental model as Jetpack Compose UI.

## Key Facts

- Only Compose-native 3D/AR SDK for Android
- Official successor to Google Sceneform (deprecated 2021)
- Built on Google Filament + ARCore
- Apache 2.0 open source
- ~5MB footprint (vs 50MB+ for Unity/Unreal)
- 15 working sample apps included
- MCP server for AI assistant integration

## Links

- Website: https://sceneview.github.io
- GitHub: https://github.com/SceneView/sceneview
- Documentation: https://sceneview.github.io/quickstart/
- API Reference: https://sceneview.github.io/api/sceneview-android/sceneview/
- Discord: https://discord.gg/UbNDDBTNqb
- Maven Central: io.github.sceneview:sceneview:3.2.0
- MCP: @sceneview/mcp (npm)

## Installation

```gradle
// 3D only
implementation("io.github.sceneview:sceneview:3.2.0")
// AR (includes 3D)
implementation("io.github.sceneview:arsceneview:3.2.0")
```

## Quick Example

```kotlin
Scene(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize()) {
    rememberModelInstance(modelLoader, "models/helmet.glb")?.let { instance ->
        ModelNode(modelInstance = instance, scaleToUnits = 1.0f, autoAnimate = true)
    }
}
```
